header_summary:
Bitcoin Core version 31.1 has been released, featuring enhancements such as bug fixes, performance improvements, and updated translations. Users should follow specific upgrade procedures based on their operating system to ensure smooth installation and can report issues or join discussions via [Bitcoin Core's GitHub](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/issues) and [mailing list](https://bitcoincore.org/en/list/announcements/join/). This update includes significant technical upgrades like a fix for the chainstate database issue and privacy improvements, enhancing overall system stability and security [Bitcoin Core Release](https://bitcoincore.org/bin/bitcoin-core-31.1/).

Discussions on cryptocurrency inflation reveal economic disparities influenced by the Cantillon effect, where entities like ASIC manufacturers benefit disproportionately. Comparisons across cryptocurrencies like Bitcoin, Bitcoin Cash, and Zcash illustrate varying impacts of inflation and transaction fees on miner revenue, highlighting the need for balanced economic strategies in blockchain technologies to ensure long-term viability and fairness [Cryptocurrency Economic Disparities](https://delvingbitcoin.org/t/addressing-the-diminishing-block-subsidy/2640/23).

Proposals to accommodate post-quantum cryptography in blockchain involve complex changes, including increased block sizes and new transaction identifiers. While integrating post-quantum cryptographic data poses challenges like increased computational demands and potential impacts on network efficiency, solutions like placing commitments within the segwit area are considered to balance advancement with operational smoothness [Blockchain and Post-Quantum Cryptography](https://delvingbitcoin.org/t/segwit-commitment-to-post-quantum-witness-data/2702).

The use of low-density parity check codes in data systems has been criticized for not addressing fundamental transmission limitations and adding unnecessary computational overhead. This ongoing issue highlights the need for more effective solutions that enhance data scalability and efficiency without imposing additional burdens [Data Transmission Inefficiencies](https://delvingbitcoin.org/t/fountain-codes-a-way-to-reduce-blockchain-storage-costs/2624/12).

recent_posts.title: Bitcoin Core v31.1 Released
recent_posts.summary:
- Bitcoin Core version 31.1 is available for download, featuring bug fixes and performance enhancements.
- Installation guidelines vary by operating system, requiring a complete shutdown of any older application version.
- The update includes significant technical improvements for enhanced stability and privacy.

recent_posts.title: Addressing the Diminishing Block Subsidy
recent_posts.summary:
- Inflation in cryptocurrency models often leads to economic disparities due to the Cantillon effect.
- Crypto mining inflation primarily benefits those invested in ASIC manufacturing by increasing hashrate.
- Differences in inflation and fees across cryptocurrencies significantly impact miner revenue and economic fairness.

recent_posts.title: Segwit commitment to post-quantum witness data?
recent_posts.summary: - Enhancing blockchain for post-quantum cryptography requires increasing block size and structure adjustments.  - New pqwtxid requires updates to mining, network infrastructure, and P2P compatibility.  - Alternative solution integrates pqdata in segwit without changing transaction identifiers significantly.

recent_posts.title: Fountain Codes: a way to reduce blockchain storage costs
recent_posts.summary:
- Low-density parity check codes do not effectively address key data transmission limits.
- These methods add extra computational work that may slow down processes.
- Peer limits restrict miner sharing, impacting data system scalability and functionality.

active_posts.title: DahLIAS: Discrete Logarithm-Based Interactive Aggregate Signatures
active_posts.summary: - DahLIAS introduces a constant-size, secp256k1-compatible aggregate signature scheme.  - It enhances transaction efficiency in Bitcoin by supporting distinct message signatures.  - The scheme is secure, efficient, and supports rapid verification processes.

active_posts.title: Giving teeth to expected EC disabling: P2XX(-T)(-ML)
active_posts.summary:
- Bitcoin developers explore disabling EC opcodes to enhance security against quantum threats.
- Two proposals, Tripwire and Miner Lockdown, aim to automate this security enhancement.
- Integration into consensus rules ensures reliable enactment of these protections.

active_posts.title: BIP draft: Full-Aggregation of BIP 340 Signatures
active_posts.summary:
- A new Bitcoin Improvement Proposal targets efficient signature aggregation.
- Jonas Nick and others developed the proposal for enhanced transaction efficiency.
- Stakeholders are invited to review and discuss the proposal via GitHub links.

active_posts.title: [BIP Draft] Segregated Data: a prunable, script-isolated block region for data carriage
active_posts.summary:
- SegData proposes a new Bitcoin block storage method using a soft-fork for efficient data handling.
- The system is designed for optional data storage costs and allows pruning by non-retaining nodes.
- Open questions remain about implementation details, impacting transaction weight and privacy issues.

active_posts.title: Public key recovery for EC leaves in P2MR (BIP-360)
active_posts.summary:
- The proposed BIP-360 optimization introduces recoverable EC leaves to reduce witness sizes.
- This method allows public key recovery from signatures, avoiding larger witness sizes and cryptographic vulnerabilities.
- Changes in witness validation and nonce generation processes are specified to enhance security and efficiency.

active_posts.title: State of the transaction privacy work in Bitcoin
active_posts.summary:
- Transaction privacy in Bitcoin focuses on sidechains, L2s, and wallet integrations.
- Cross-Input Signature Aggregation could lower CoinJoin costs without boosting privacy.
- Current privacy efforts prioritize regulatory compatibility and minimal protocol changes.

today_in_history_posts.title: BIP 118 and SIGHASH_ANYPREVOUT
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- BIP 118 was updated to incorporate the ANYPREVOUT feature and improve adaptability.
- Chaperone signatures were removed but can still be implemented as needed by users.
- The community is encouraged to review, discuss, and refine the draft PR on GitHub.

today_in_history_posts.title: Dynamic Commitments: Upgrading Channels Without On-Chain Transactions
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Rusty proposes adding `commit_switch` and `commit_switch_reply` to facilitate upgrades.
- He suggests a linear progression of channel types, favoring newer over older.
- Upgrades use existing bits and depend on feature matching between parties.

today_in_history_posts.title: Thoughts on scaling and consensus changes (2023)
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Bitcoin aims to scale by creating 50,000 off-chain "bitcoin banks."- These entities range from federated sidechains to time-sensitive smart contract coinpools.
- Managing scalability involves new tools and strategies for second-layer solutions.
